name: an empty trace has no moments
intent: With no fills there is no opening, no closing, and no gap to measure; all three are null.

name: a single fill is both the first to fall and the last square, with no turning point
intent: One entry is the min and the max by at, so firstToFall equals lastSquare (atSeconds 0 relative to itself). turningPoint needs two entries to have a gap, so it is null.

name: a long stall then a burst: largest gap is the stall, the fill that ends it is the break, next 30s is the burst
intent: Fills at 0, 5s, 60s, 61s, 62s. firstToFall is the earliest at, lastSquare the latest. The largest consecutive gap is 5s->60s (55s), so stallSeconds 55, breakSeconds 60 (the entry that ends the gap), and burst 3 (the entries at 60s, 61s, 62s, all within [60s, 90s]). Pins the turning point against BURST_WINDOW = 30s.

name: two fills at the same instant: first to fall breaks the tie by min seq
intent: Two entries share the minimum at; firstToFall takes the min seq (u1 at seq 1, not u2 at seq 2). lastSquare is the latest at. The largest gap is the pause to the last fill.

name: COMPOSED (D29): the overnight gap is not the turning point; the stall is measured within sittings
intent: A composed case: given carries events and solution beside the trace, and given.trace IS solveTrace(collapseIdle(events), solution), the pipeline equality the Wave 11.2 reader asserts (today's reader runs the reducer on the trace as usual). On the wall clock the largest gap is the ten-hour night (36,000,000 ms >= SITTING_GAP_MS, e5 to e6); the collapse makes that gap exactly zero, so the stall scan sees the largest WITHIN-sitting gap instead: the 180 s pause broken at active 200 s, burst 2 inside BURST_WINDOW. lastSquare reads active seconds (270, not ~36270).
